subject: Project Management
title: The Availability Heuristic
description: A quick, practical guide to The Availability Heuristic.

hook.text: We judge risk based on what we can easily remember — not on what's statistically likely.

buildup.text: A recent project failure makes us overestimate that type of risk. No recent failures? We underestimate.

discovery.text: The availability heuristic means vivid, recent events dominate our risk assessment over quiet, base-rate data.

twist.text: After 9/11, people drove instead of flying — even though driving was statistically far more dangerous.

climax.text: In projects, we over-prepare for last year's crisis and ignore tomorrow's likely failure.

punchline.text: We prepare for the disaster we remember, not the one coming.

quiz.question: How does the availability heuristic distort risk assessment?
options: Recent or vivid events feel more likely than they statistically are
It makes us ignore all risks
